sections.paragraph:
with distinct accents צָר and art. הַצָּר; with suff. צָרִי; pl. צָרִים, צָרֵי (from the root צָרַר).-

(1) an adversary, an enemy (see the root No. 4), i.q. אֹויֵב; but, besides Num 10:9, only poet. ( Job 16:9 Deu 32:27 Psa 81:15 Lam 1:7 Isa 9:10); and in the later books, Est 7:4, Neh 9:27.

(2) distress, affliction (see the root No. 5), Psa 4:2 44:11 78:42 . בַּצַּר לִי when I am in distress, Psa 18:7 66:14 106:44 102:3 , בְּיֹום צַר לִי id. Fem. צָרָה id. which see.

(3) a stone (so called as being compact and hard; i.q. צוּר No. 1), Isa 5:28.
